compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The live Jira/LLM external tier (`tests/external/`) runs only on `workflow_dispatch`
(`.github/workflows/external-integration.yml`), which sets `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1` and maps
`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`OPENAI_API_KEY` but **no `JIRA_*` secrets** and does **not install the
`acli` binary** ŌĆö and `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py::_live_jira_ready()` gates on BOTH
(`creds and shutil.which("acli")`). So the live Jira round-trip can only ever SKIP: Jira
API/SDK drift merges through normal CI with no real integration signal, and nothing fails a
run in which the whole external tier silently skips. VALIDATED.

Correction to the earlier framing: the live test is NOT read-only ŌĆö it calls `create_issue`
twice + `set_relationship`, then in a `try/finally` deletes every artifact it created (links
then issues). It makes REAL, self-cleaning Jira mutations against whatever project the creds
point at, so it requires a DEDICATED disposable test project, not a shared one.

## What
1. **Workflow** (`external-integration.yml`): add an `on.schedule` trigger with the exact cron
   `0 6 * * 1` (Mondays 06:00 UTC, weekly) alongside `workflow_dispatch`, and MIRROR the
   established `reconcile-bridge.yml` convention
   EXACTLY (not an ad-hoc variant):
   - Jira env, sourced the SAME way reconcile-bridge does: `JIRA_URL: ${{ vars.JIRA_URL }}`,
     `JIRA_USER: ${{ vars.JIRA_USER }}` (repo VARIABLES, not secrets), and
     `JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}` (the only secret), and `JIRA_PROJECT: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT }}` (the live Jira tests read it for the target project).
   - the `acli` install, reusing reconcile-bridge's THREE companion steps verbatim in shape:
     the `cache-acli` cache step, the `Download + verify + install acli` step (pinned
     `vars.ACLI_VERSION` + `vars.ACLI_SHA256`, sha256-checked), AND the `Add acli to PATH and
     authenticate` step exactly as reconcile-bridge does it: `sudo ln -sf "$HOME/.acli/acli"
     /usr/local/bin/acli` (symlink the binary into the on-PATH `/usr/local/bin`, NOT
     `$GITHUB_PATH`), then `echo "$JIRA_API_TOKEN" | acli jira auth login --site "$JIRA_URL"
     --email "$JIRA_USER" --token`. Without this step `shutil.which("acli")` and the live auth
     both fail, so the Jira tests still skip.
2. **Fail-on-all-skip Jira canary** ŌĆö deterministic, marker-based (NOT skip-string matching,
   which is fragile across the tier's many distinct skip reasons), and SELF-MAINTAINING (no
   hardcoded module list, so a future Jira module is auto-covered). All Jira live modules share
   one gate signature: a module-level `_live_jira_ready()` + `_skip = skipif(not
   _live_jira_ready(), ŌĆ”)` (currently `test_link_sync_live.py`, `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`,
   `test_reconcile_live.py`, `test_verified_fake_contract_live.py`, but the hook keys off the
   signature, not this list).
   - Register a `jira_live` marker (pyproject `[tool.pytest.ini_options].markers`).
   - In `tests/external/conftest.py`, a `pytest_collection_modifyitems` hook AUTO-APPLIES the
     `jira_live` marker to every collected item whose `item.module` defines `_live_jira_ready`
     (i.e. every Jira-gated module, present or future) ŌĆö no per-file `@pytest.mark` edits, no
     enumeration to drift.
   - A `pytest_sessionfinish(session, exitstatus)` hook (which sets a nonzero `session.exitstatus` to fail the run) that, when
     `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` is truthy, REPORTS the `jira_live` executed-vs-skipped counts and
     FAILS the session (nonzero exit) iff Ōēź1 `jira_live` test was COLLECTED but ZERO executed
     (passed or failed) ŌĆö exactly the "Jira secrets/acli missing, no real signal" condition.
     Membership is the marker (deterministic), so unrelated always-on tests
     (`test_model_requests_enabled`, `test_deps_live`) neither mask nor trip it.
   - When `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` is unset (the default suite, tier inert via the opt-in guard),
     the hook is a no-op.
3. **Tests**:
   - a behavioral test of the fail-on-all-skip hook over SIMULATED session reports: a session
     where all `jira_live` tests skipped (with `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1`) makes the hook signal
     failure; a session where Ōēź1 `jira_live` test executed passes; and an unset
     `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` is a no-op. (Drive the hook function directly with constructed
     report objects ŌĆö no live creds needed.)
   - a config-as-artifact test asserting `external-integration.yml` declares an `on.schedule`
     trigger, maps `JIRA_URL`/`JIRA_USER`/`JIRA_API_TOKEN` into the external job env, AND
     installs `acli` / puts it on PATH (a drift guard mirroring the repo's YAML-artifact tests).

## Scope
- `.github/workflows/external-integration.yml` ŌĆö schedule + JIRA_* env + acli install/PATH/auth.
- `pyproject.toml` ŌĆö register the `jira_live` pytest marker.
- `tests/external/conftest.py` ŌĆö register-free auto-marking (`pytest_collection_modifyitems`
  keyed on `_live_jira_ready`) + the marker-scoped fail-on-all-skip session hook + report.
- A test module (e.g. `tests/unit/test_external_canary.py`) ŌĆö the hook behavior + workflow wiring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The workflow declares `on.schedule` with cron `0 6 * * 1`; sources `JIRA_URL`/`JIRA_USER`/`JIRA_PROJECT`
      from `vars.*` and `JIRA_API_TOKEN` from `secrets.*` (matching reconcile-bridge.yml); and
      installs `acli` + symlinks it onto PATH + authenticates (the cache / download-verify-install
      / add-to-PATH-and-authenticate steps). A config-as-artifact test pins the cron, the Jira
      env wiring, and the acli PATH/auth step.
- [ ] A `jira_live` marker is registered and AUTO-APPLIED (via a collection hook keyed on
      `_live_jira_ready`) to every Jira-gated live module; the
      conftest hook FAILS an external session (with `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1`) iff Ōēź1 `jira_live`
      test was collected but zero executed (all skipped), PASSES when a `jira_live` test
      executed, and is a no-op when `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` is unset ŌĆö asserted by a behavioral
      test driving the hook over constructed report objects.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The operator configures the `JIRA_URL`/`JIRA_USER` repo VARIABLES,
      the `JIRA_API_TOKEN` SECRET, and the `ACLI_VERSION`/`ACLI_SHA256` repo variables, and
      provisions a DEDICATED disposable Jira test project (these require GitHub repo-admin + a
      Jira project and cannot be done from the code change). CONCRETE evidence to record as a
      comment on this ticket: the URL of a manual `workflow_dispatch` run of the
      `external-integration` workflow whose `external` job is GREEN with the live Jira
      round-trip test EXECUTED (passed, not skipped) ŌĆö i.e. the fail-on-all-skip canary did NOT
      fire ŌĆö plus the run id/timestamp. A blank "done" without that run URL does not satisfy it.
- [ ] `make lint && make test` green.

file_impact.reason: add schedule + wire JIRA_* secrets; fail-on-all-skip
report executed-vs-skipped counts; fail if all skip

compiled_state.title: Scheduled external contract canary that fails when all live tests skip
